preFix.title: Fitting a dual-flush conversion kit in a cistern
preFix.subtitle: Replace a single-flush siphon with a dual-flush valve to reduce water use
preFix.excerpt: How to remove the existing single-flush siphon or flap valve from a close-coupled or wall-hung cistern and fit a dual-flush conversion kit, including isolating the water supply, draining the cistern, removing the old mechanism, fitting the new dual-flush valve body and actuator plate, and adjusting the flush volumes.
preFix.sourceNotes: Collins Complete DIY Manual. Fluidmaster and Thomas Dudley technical installation guides. Water Regulations Advisory Scheme (WRAS) guidance on cistern fittings.

content.text: Converting a single-flush siphon to a

is a straightforward task that requires no soldering and no pipe cutting. The main step before any work is isolating the water supply by closing the isolation valve on the cold supply pipe to the cistern. Confirm isolation worked by attempting to flush: the cistern should not refill.
Step 1, isolate and drain the cistern

Close the isolation valve on the cold supply to the cistern by turning the flat-blade screw slot a quarter turn with a screwdriver until it is crosswise to the pipe. Flush to empty the cistern, then sponge out any remaining water. Remove the cistern lid and set it aside.
Step 2, remove the old flush siphon

is secured by a large plastic nut on the underside of the cistern base. Grip the nut from below with the water-pump pliers and unscrew it anticlockwise. Lift the siphon up and out of the cistern. Disconnect the flush lever link before the siphon is fully removed. On some cisterns, the siphon is also retained by a clip or tie to the fill valve: disconnect this if present.

Fit the new dual-flush valve body into the outlet hole in the cistern base. Feed the tail through the base and hand-tighten the retaining nut from below. Do not overtighten: the nut seals against a rubber washer and overtightening cracks the cistern base. Attach the fill hose if the kit includes a replacement
; if reusing the existing fill valve, check that it is connected and the float arm is not fouling the new valve body.

Remove the cistern lid. Insert the actuator rod from the dual-flush valve up through the hole in the lid. Fit the actuator button plate and lock it according to the manufacturer's instructions: typically a quarter-turn bayonet. Check both buttons depress and spring back cleanly.
Step 5, restore the supply and test

Reopen the isolation valve and allow the cistern to fill. Watch for any leak at the base of the new valve from below: drips indicate the base nut needs a further quarter-turn. When full, test the full-flush button and the reduced-flush button. Adjust the flush volumes on the valve body if the conversion kit includes adjustment clips: most do, allowing the reduced flush to be set between 2 and 4 litres.

Wall-hung cisterns with concealed frames use a different conversion kit. It attaches to the flush plate rather than entering the cistern from above. These cisterns usually already have a dual-flush plate. If the flush leaks or the volumes need adjusting, remove the wall flush plate to access the mechanism. No internal cistern access is needed.

attrs.fix: Isolate and drain the cistern. Remove the valve, check the washer is seated flat, refit, and tighten the base nut to hand-tight plus a further quarter turn. No tools should be needed beyond firm hand pressure.
attrs.cause: Base nut not sufficiently tightened; rubber washer displaced or twisted during installation.
attrs.symptom: Cistern leaks from the base of the flush valve after fitting

attrs.fix: Confirm the isolation valve is fully open. If slow refill persists, remove the fill valve cap, inspect the screen, and rinse clear under the tap.
attrs.cause: Isolation valve only partially opened; debris in the fill valve inlet screen.
attrs.symptom: Cistern takes a long time to refill after flushing

attrs.fix: Drain the cistern and adjust the full-flush volume clip on the valve body to maximum. Test and readjust as needed.
attrs.cause: Flush volume set too low on the new valve; valve not fully opening.
attrs.symptom: Toilet does not flush fully on the full-flush button

initialFindings.message: PATTERN tutorial has no Method/Steps section and no orderedList — sequential instructions missing
